evals.prompt: I'm building a payment processing service in my Laravel app. Write a ProcessPayment class with a handle() method that processes a payment for a given order ID, ensuring only one payment attempt can run at a time per order. Use the best available Laravel API for atomic locking.
evals.expected_output: Agent searches docs (with a low token limit for this focused lookup) then writes the implementation
expectations: The agent describes running a search query against the Laravel documentation before writing code
The search command specifies --token-limit of 1500 or less
The solution uses a Cache facade method or class for atomic locking
The agent explicitly states it is basing the implementation on what the documentation says

evals.prompt: My Laravel app has an Order model with customer and items relationships. Iterating orders and accessing order->customer causes N+1 queries. Show me how to fix this globally so ALL model relationship access is automatically optimized, without manually adding with() to every query.
evals.expected_output: Agent searches docs (with a low token limit) then writes the implementation
expectations: The agent describes running a search query against the Laravel documentation before writing code
The search command specifies --token-limit of 1500 or less
The solution addresses the N+1 problem globally, not just by suggesting with() on a single query
The agent explicitly states it is basing the implementation on what the documentation says

evals.prompt: Write a Laravel helper method that filters a list of filenames, keeping only those that do NOT start with 'draft_' or 'temp_'. Use the most readable current Laravel string helper API.
evals.expected_output: Agent searches docs (with a low token limit for this quick method lookup) then writes the implementation
expectations: The agent describes running a search query against the Laravel documentation before writing code
The search command specifies --token-limit of 1000 or less
The solution uses a Laravel Str helper or Stringable method rather than plain PHP string functions
The agent explicitly states it is basing the implementation on what the documentation says

expectations: The agent describes running a search query against the Laravel documentation before writing code

evals.prompt: I need to quickly check the exact method signature for how to paginate an Eloquent query in Laravel. Just look it up in the docs.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses a minimal token limit for this quick syntax check
expectations: The agent describes running a search query against the Laravel documentation
The search command specifies --token-limit of 800 or less for this quick syntax check
The agent uses a single search call, not multiple separate searches for this simple lookup
The agent uses a 2-4 word technical query, not a natural language question

evals.prompt: I need to implement login, registration, and password reset in a Laravel app. Search the docs for the relevant packages and approaches.
evals.expected_output: Agent batches related queries in one call and uses an appropriate limit for multi-topic research
expectations: The agent describes running a search query against the Laravel documentation
The agent batches multiple related terms into a single search.py call rather than making separate calls for each topic
The search command specifies --token-limit of 2000 or less
The agent does NOT use 3000 as the default without explicit justification for needing that much
